commands.author: Jerry-Xin

target.title: fix(tts): defer text settlement for final-mode TTS to eliminate churn (#83511)
target.body:
## Summary

When `messages.tts.mode = "final"`, the Telegram bot exhibited text "churn" — users would briefly see a text message appear, then it would be deleted and replaced by a voice note with the same text as caption. This happened on **both** the ACP dispatch path and the non-ACP dispatch path (`dispatch-from-config.ts`).

This PR eliminates the churn on **both paths** by introducing a `captionedFinalText` channel capability, suppressing live block delivery and partial streaming when captioned final TTS will be used, and correctly handling tagged auto-TTS mode with a split eligibility boolean.

## Root cause

```
Before (main):
  1. Block text / partial streaming delivers text to user immediately
  2. TTS synthesis runs (2-5 seconds)
  3. Voice note + caption delivered as separate message
  Result: user sees text first, then voice note = churn (#83511)

After (this PR):
  1. willUseCaptionedFinalTts = true (channel supports captioned voice + final TTS mode)
  2. Partial streaming suppressed, block text delivery suppressed
  3. TTS synthesis runs, text accumulated silently
  4. Voice note + caption delivered as single message (or text fallback if TTS fails)
  Result: user sees only the voice note with caption = no churn
```

### Tagged auto-TTS mode

When `messages.tts.auto: "tagged"`, TTS only fires for replies containing `[[tts:...]]` directives. Three additional issues were discovered and fixed:

1. **Split boolean**: `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` was doing two jobs — gating live text suppression AND caption eligibility. Split into `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` (live text suppression, excludes tagged) and `canCaptionFinalTts` (caption eligibility, includes tagged).

2. **Block TTS gate**: The accumulated block TTS fallback was gated on `replies.length === 0`, but Anthropic streaming returns both block callbacks and a final reply payload. Changed to gate on `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` and `queuedFinal` instead.

3. **Partial streaming leak**: `onPartialReply` was active for tagged mode, sending raw `[[tts:text]]...[[/tts:text]]` directive text to Telegram during streaming, creating a duplicate text message before the voice+caption arrived. Fixed by gating `onPartialReply` with `canCaptionFinalTts` (covers both always and tagged modes) instead of `willUseCaptionedFinalTts`.

### Media block preservation

When captioned final TTS is active, block suppression is narrowed to **text-only** payloads. Blocks containing media (`mediaUrl`, `mediaUrls`), `presentation`, `interactive`, or `channelData` are delivered immediately with their text stripped (to avoid duplicating the caption). This applies to both ACP and non-ACP dispatch paths.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts` | `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` + `canCaptionFinalTts`: suppress partial streaming and block text for both always and tagged modes; narrow block suppression to text-only payloads (media blocks delivered with text stripped); `sendFinalPayload` returns `deliveredMedia`; block TTS gate changed from `replies.length === 0` to `!deliveredFinalTtsMedia && !queuedFinal` |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts` | Regression tests: tagged directive-only voice+caption, tagged+always both-block-and-final delivery, partial suppression for tagged TTS on caption-capable channels, plain tagged block delivery, media block delivery with text stripped, text-only block suppression |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts` | `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` gate: suppress ACP block delivery and defer text settlement; `canCaptionFinalTts` for tagged mode; `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` tracking |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.ts` | `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` state tracking + `hasDeliveredFinalTtsMedia()` accessor; narrow `suppressBlockUserDelivery` to text-only blocks (media blocks delivered with text stripped) |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.test.ts` | Regression tests: media block delivery with text stripped under `suppressBlockUserDelivery`, text-only block suppression |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts` | Regression tests: deferred settlement, block suppression, tagged streaming, tagged directive TTS, ACP live text deferral |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/acp-projector.ts` | `deferLiveTextUntilFinalFlush` option to suppress live text flushing for tagged TTS |
| `src/tts/directives.ts` | `resolveDirectiveOnlyTtsCaptionText` parser helper to extract clean caption from directive-only responses |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.ts` | `captionedFinalText: true` capability declaration |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.test.ts` | Test for capability declaration |
| `src/channels/plugins/types.core.ts` | JSDoc for `captionedFinalText` capability contract |
| `src/channels/plugins/tts-capabilities.test.ts` | Capability seam test |
| `docs/tools/tts.md` | Document `captionedFinalText` capability: opt-in criteria via `capabilities.tts.voice`, tagged mode behavior, text-only fallback, plugin API snippet |
| `docs/plugins/sdk-channel-plugins.md` | Document `captionedFinalText` capability in channel plugin SDK guide: opt-in declaration, media block preservation, fallback behavior, tagged mode |
| `docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256` | Updated SDK API baseline hash |

## Tests

```
$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts
 ✓ 202 passed (202)

$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.test.ts
 ✓ 29 passed (29)

$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
 ✓ 50 passed (50)

$ npx vitest run extensions/telegram/src/shared.test.ts
 ✓ 11 passed (11)

$ npx vitest run src/channels/plugins/tts-capabilities.test.ts
 ✓ 1 passed (1)

Total: 293 tests passed across 5 test suites
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Telegram TTS final-mode text-before-voice churn on both ACP and non-ACP dispatch paths, plus tagged mode partial preview leaking raw `[[tts:text]]` directives, plus media block suppression during captioned final TTS.

- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout at commit `05b4483a8f`, macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0. Live Telegram bot (`@thomas_fu_bot`) with Microsoft TTS (`zh-CN-XiaoxiaoNeural`), `messages.tts.mode: "final"`.

- **Failing proof before fix (live Telegram)**:

  ![Before fix — separate text message + voice message (churn)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Jerry-Xin/openclaw/proof-images-83988/.proof-images/83988-before-fix.png)

  User sent "hello" to the Telegram bot. The bot responded with **two separate messages**: a text message AND a voice note — instead of a single voice note with caption.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  1. Built and installed from current head: `npm run build && npm pack && npm install -g openclaw-2026.6.2.tgz`
  2. Registered and restarted Gateway: `openclaw gateway install && openclaw gateway restart`
  3. Tested with `messages.tts.auto: "always"` and `messages.tts.auto: "tagged"` — sent plain messages and directive-bearing messages to `@thomas_fu_bot` on Telegram

- **Evidence after fix**: Always mode (`messages.tts.auto: "always"`) at commit `05b4483a8f`:

  ![Always mode — voice with caption, single message](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Jerry-Xin/openclaw/proof-images-83988/.proof-images/83988-always-mode.png)

  Always-mode replies produce a single voice note with caption text. No text churn, no separate text bubble.

- **Evidence — Media block preservation with captioned final TTS at commit `05b4483a8f`**:

  ![Media block preserved — image delivered + voice note with caption](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Jerry-Xin/openclaw/proof-images-83988/.proof-images/83988-media-block.png)

  When the bot generates an image during a TTS-enabled conversation (`auto: "always"`), the image is delivered immediately while text is deferred to the voice note caption. No media blocks are suppressed, and caption text does not duplicate as a separate text message.

- **Observed result after fix**: Always TTS auto mode delivers voice notes as single messages with caption text on Telegram. Media blocks (images, files) are preserved and delivered immediately with text stripped. No text flash, no duplicate messages, no media loss.

- **What was not tested**: Other TTS providers (only Microsoft TTS tested live). Group chat TTS (tested in DM only). Other channels with `captionedFinalText` capability (only Telegram tested). Tagged mode live proof pending — covered by unit tests (partial suppression + directive-only caption regression tests added).

Closes #83511

commands.author: zeroaltitude

target.title: perf(qmd): persistent export-state cache + stat fast path in exportSessions
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** For agents with thousands of session transcripts, `QmdMemoryManager.exportSessions()` was the dominant gateway CPU consumer. Every boot and every QMD sync interval walked every session JSONL and called `buildSessionEntry()` per file, which performs full read + parse + redaction + hash/render work even when the transcript export was unchanged.
- **Why it matters:** The previous in-memory skip state only avoided some markdown rewrites after a manager instance had already rebuilt each entry. It did not survive gateway restart, so cold starts and periodic syncs could still create large CPU and GC spikes on production-sized transcript sets.
- **What changed:**
  1. Added a per-agent SQLite cache table, `qmd_session_export_cache`, in `openclaw-agent.sqlite`, keyed by `(session_file, export_dir, render_version)`.
  2. Added a stat + full-content-fingerprint fast path before `buildSessionEntry()`. When cached `size`, `mtimeMs`, `ino`, full content fingerprint, and cached markdown target all match, QMD skips the expensive full-session rebuild.
  3. Preserved the slow path for changed transcripts, missing markdown targets, mismatched cached targets, retention pruning, export-dir changes, and render-version changes.
  4. Kept raw per-agent DB/Kysely access inside the core state owner and exposed only QMD-specific cache helpers through a private local-only SDK subpath, `openclaw/plugin-sdk/memory-core-qmd-export-cache-internal`, scoped to the bundled `memory-core` plugin.
  5. Batch-loaded the current export scope cache rows once per `exportSessions()` pass and reused an in-memory lookup map, so large transcript sets do not reopen/refresh the per-agent DB accessor once per session file.
  6. Excluded the generated private QMD SDK helper artifacts from npm package output: `!dist/plugin-sdk/memory-core-qmd-export-cache-internal.js` and `!dist/plugin-sdk/memory-core-qmd-export-cache-internal.d.ts`.

**Scope boundary:** `renderSessionMarkdown()`, session redaction, line wrapping, and the markdown format QMD consumes are unchanged. `buildSessionEntry()` remains the authoritative slow path. The cache is scoped by export directory and `SESSION_EXPORT_RENDER_VERSION`, so config moves and render-byte changes invalidate cleanly.

**Effect on Eddie's main agent (4,052 sessions / 158 MB):**
- Boot QMD sync: **312s -> single-digit seconds**
- Interval QMD sync: **38s -> ~50-500 ms**
- The every-5-min CPU spike + GC tail collapses

## Linked Issue

- Closes openclaw-ys0 (Beads tracking issue)

## Test Coverage

New and updated tests cover the cache contract:

- `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts`
  - persists session-export state across manager restarts
  - rebuilds after same-size transcript rewrites, proving content changes are not hidden by matching stat fields
  - ignores cache rows scoped to a previous `exportDir`
  - rebuilds markdown when the cached target has been deleted
  - rebuilds the expected markdown when a current-scope cache row points at another target
  - preserves existing session-export behavior for checkpoint/session filtering
- `src/state/openclaw-agent-db.test.ts`
  - schema shape includes the new derived-state cache table
  - per-agent DB schema metadata and `user_version` remain at version 1
  - newer-schema refusal still protects downgrade safety
- `test/release-check.test.ts`
  - package output rejects private local-only plugin SDK artifacts
  - package `files` exclusions include every private local-only SDK artifact, including the new QMD cache helper

## Tradeoffs / Known Caveats

- The fast path still uses stat identity to avoid hashing obviously changed files, but it verifies a full SHA-1 content fingerprint before skipping the rebuild. Same-size rewrites with preserved or seeded stat identity do not suppress updated markdown.
- `SESSION_EXPORT_RENDER_VERSION` must be bumped when changing `renderSessionMarkdown()`, redaction, line wrapping, or any other logic that changes the bytes written to `qmd/sessions/*.md`.
- The cache table is derived state; the per-agent DB `user_version` remains at 1 for rollback safety.
- No hand-written `CHANGELOG.md` entry is included; current repo policy keeps release notes out of normal PRs and derives them during release generation.

## Real Behavior Proof

- **Behavior addressed:** QMD session export persists its unchanged-transcript fast-path state in the per-agent SQLite DB without exposing raw per-agent DB/Kysely access through the public plugin SDK. It must still rebuild markdown when a cached target is missing, when the cached target points elsewhere, and when stat identity matches but the transcript content fingerprint changed. The private QMD SDK artifact must also stay out of package output.
- **Real environment tested:** Current PR head `eb71a680cc4339778c93130d923833f98a238b30` in the `perf/qmd-export-stat-fast-path` worktree, after merging current `openclaw/main` and cleaning unrelated formatter-only merge churn, using the real `QmdMemoryManager`, real `resolveMemoryBackendConfig`, the private local-only QMD cache subpath, real per-agent `openclaw-agent.sqlite` storage, and local `qmd 2.1.0` on PATH. The proof ran against an isolated `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` under `/tmp`; no Vitest harness or mocked QMD export cache was used.
- **Exact steps run after this patch:** `node --import tsx --input-type=module` from the topic worktree with an inline driver. The driver created one session JSONL, ran `QmdMemoryManager.sync({ reason: "manual" })` across fresh manager instances, inspected and seeded `qmd_session_export_cache` through the private QMD helper subpath, rewrote the transcript with a deliberately stale fingerprint row whose size/mtime/inode matched the source, deleted the generated markdown target, and verified the next sync rebuilt it.
- **Evidence after fix:** Captured terminal output from the live run:

```text
current head: eb71a680cc4339778c93130d923833f98a238b30
qmd version: qmd 2.1.0
=== Phase 1: first sync writes markdown and SQLite cache ===
[memory] qmd manager initialized for agent "main" mode=full collections=2 durationMs=899
[memory] qmd sync completed for agent "main" reason=manual durationMs=377
sync 1: sync done in 380 ms
cache target is export file: true
fingerprint length: 40
=== Phase 2: unchanged sync uses fast path ===
[memory] qmd manager initialized for agent "main" mode=full collections=2 durationMs=828
[memory] qmd sync completed for agent "main" reason=manual durationMs=299
sync 2: sync done in 301 ms
export mtime unchanged: true
export size unchanged: true
=== Phase 3: matching stat row with stale content fingerprint rebuilds ===
seeded cache size matches source: true
seeded cache mtime matches source: true
seeded cache ino matches source: true
[memory] qmd manager initialized for agent "main" mode=full collections=2 durationMs=813
[memory] qmd sync completed for agent "main" reason=manual durationMs=306
sync 3: sync done in 308 ms
export mtime newer: true
cache fingerprint changed: true
cache fingerprint length after rebuild: 40
=== Phase 4: delete markdown while SQLite cache survives ===
export exists after delete: false
sqlite cache row still present: true
[memory] qmd manager initialized for agent "main" mode=full collections=2 durationMs=818
[memory] qmd sync completed for agent "main" reason=manual durationMs=307
sync 4: sync done in 309 ms
rebuilt export exists: true
cache target restored to export file: true

All runtime assertions passed.
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The first sync wrote markdown and a SQLite cache row with the expected derived target and 40-character fingerprint. The second sync preserved markdown mtime/size, proving unchanged input took the persisted fast path. The third sync rebuilt after a same-size rewrite even though the seeded cache row matched the source stat fields, proving the full-content fingerprint prevents stale skips. After deleting markdown while leaving SQLite cache intact, the fourth sync regenerated the target and restored the cache target to the expected export file.
- **What was not tested:** I did not rerun a benchmark against Eddie's full 4,052-session production corpus after the SDK-boundary rewrite, base-branch merge, package exclusion fix, cached-target validation fix, and batch-read follow-up. The proof above validates the current SQLite cache behavior and regression paths on real runtime code; the original production-sized timing numbers remain observational context.

## Verification

Current head `eb71a680cc4339778c93130d923833f98a238b30` includes:

- `3468c322a6` (`fix(qmd): exclude private cache SDK artifact from package`)
- signed base-refresh merges from current `openclaw/main` to clear the PR's dirty/conflicting merge state

commands.author: Jerry-Xin

target.title: fix: prevent silent message loss from EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError
target.body:
## Summary

When the OpenAI SDK retried an `ECONNRESET` internally (default `maxRetries=2`), the session write lock was released during the retry window. Incoming steering messages could then modify the transcript without updating the fence, causing a fingerprint mismatch on `reacquireAfterPrompt()`. The resulting `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` was caught by `model-fallback.ts` which aborted the run, and the outer reply pipeline produced no user-visible output — the message was silently lost.

## Root Cause Chain

```
API ECONNRESET
  → OpenAI SDK default maxRetries=2, internal retry
  → session write lock released during retry window
  → incoming message arrives via queueHandle.queueMessage
  → activeSession.steer writes directly (bypassing withOwnedSessionTranscriptWrites)
  → transcript changes without updating fence
  → original attempt reacquireAfterPrompt() fingerprint mismatch
  → EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError thrown
  → model-fallback.ts aborts the run
  → outer layer hits generic failure branch
  → assistantTexts/payloads never enter buildReplyPayloads()
  → message silently lost (user sees generic error or nothing)
```

## Fix

Five coordinated changes that address the bug at multiple layers:

### 1. Wrap steering in `withOwnedSessionTranscriptWrites` (`attempt.ts`)
Steering writes that arrive during prompt lock release now go through `withOwnedSessionTranscriptWrites`, keeping fence tracking consistent and preventing the fingerprint mismatch that triggers the takeover error.

### 2. Dedicated error handler for takeover (`agent-runner-execution.ts`, `failover-error.ts`)
Added `isEmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError()` export and a dedicated catch block in `runAgentTurnWithFallback`. Instead of falling into the generic failure branch (silent loss), users now receive a clear message asking them to resend. When a takeover wraps a preserved prompt error (billing, rate-limit, context-overflow), the original error is unwrapped and classified normally — only pure takeover errors return resend guidance.

### 3. Scope SDK retry disable to embedded prompt lock path (`attempt.ts`, `attempt.session-lock.ts`)
Extracted `installEmbeddedPromptRetryDefault` helper that wraps the session's `streamFn` with `{ maxRetries: 0, ...options }` — the `0` is a **default** that goes before the caller options spread, so `settings.retry.provider.maxRetries` (surfaced via `options.maxRetries` per the chain in `sdk.ts:360`) takes precedence when explicitly configured. Non-embedded callers retain the SDK default retry behavior (maxRetries=2).

### 4. Forward maxRetries through native OpenAI transport (`openai-transport-stream.ts`)
`buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions` previously accepted only `model` and `signal`, dropping the `maxRetries` injected by `installEmbeddedPromptRetryDefault`. Added `maxRetries` parameter and forwarded it through all three native transport call sites (Responses, Azure Responses, Completions). When `maxRetries` is the only option present, the function now returns an object instead of `undefined` so the SDK receives the override.

### 5. Phase tracking in `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` (`attempt.session-lock.ts`)
Added a `phase` field (`prompt_reacquire`, `cleanup`, `write`, `fence_check`) for better diagnostics when this error does occur. Backward compatible — the phase parameter is optional with existing call sites unchanged.

## Real behavior proof

> **Refreshed at current head `bf4c277c9b` (supersedes the v6 proof below, which targeted older head `2fc3ecee2d`).**

- **Behavior addressed**: A session takeover during the embedded prompt-lock release window must not silently drop the user's reply, and the injected embedded retry default must refresh with the latest configured `settings.retry.provider.maxRetries` across repeated installs (not freeze the first-captured value).
- **Real environment tested**: Local patched checkout at `bf4c277c9b`, macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`, freshly installed deps (`pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`).
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Ran the affected suites at this head with `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs <suite>` — the organic session-takeover regression (real on-disk session + real local HTTP model server + real OpenAI-completions transport + real prompt-lock fence), the retry-default live-ref unit suite (V1–V5), and the reply-runtime regression — plus `tsgo:test:src` for typecheck.
- **Evidence after fix**: Copied live terminal output captured at head `bf4c277c9b`:
```
 ✓ surfaces resend guidance after an organic EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError  724ms
 ✓ src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts (72 tests) 141ms
      Tests  72 passed (72)
      Tests  205 passed (205)
 tsgo:test:src exits 0 with no errors
```
- **Observed result after fix**: The organic session takeover now surfaces explicit resend guidance ("⚠️ Your message was interrupted because new input arrived while the model was retrying a connection error. Please resend your message.") instead of silently dropping the reply; the retry-default wrapper reads the shared live ref at call time so a repeat install refreshes the next call (including downward refresh when the configured value drops); and typecheck is clean at this head.
- **What was not tested**: The live Feishu/DeepSeek ECONNRESET timing race that originally surfaced #87180 is not reproduced here; the takeover is reproduced deterministically via a real on-disk fence-drift, which exercises the same `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` path. The CI matrix on this head was still running at proof time.


### Current-head proof (`bf4c277c9b`)

- **Behavior addressed**: A session takeover during the embedded prompt-lock release window must not silently drop the user's reply, and the injected embedded retry default must refresh with the latest configured `settings.retry.provider.maxRetries` across repeated installs (not freeze the first-captured value).
- **Real environment tested**: Local patched checkout at `bf4c277c9b`, macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`, freshly installed deps (`pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`).
- **Why refreshed**: The previous proof targeted an older head; the retry-default wrapper was reworked to read a shared live ref at call time (commit on this head), so proof is regenerated on the resulting head.

### 1. Organic session-takeover regression (real on-disk session + real local HTTP model server + real OpenAI-completions transport + real prompt-lock fence)
```
     ✓ surfaces resend guidance after an organic EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError  724ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  1 passed (1)
```
The takeover error is thrown organically by the real prompt-lock fence (a concurrent steering write rewrites the same session file mid-call), and `runAgentTurnWithFallback` surfaces resend guidance instead of dropping the reply.

### 2. Retry-default live-ref unit proof (the wrapper fix, V1–V5)
```
 ✓  agents  src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts (72 tests) 141ms
      Tests  72 passed (72)
```
Covers: refresh through the buried wrapper after lock-release wraps it; call-time live read so a repeat install changes the next call; downward refresh when the configured value drops (the original bug's essence); shared ref identity across both install orders; and no wrapper stacking on repeated installs.

### 3. Reply-runtime regression (no behavior regression on the takeover catch path)
```
      Tests  205 passed (205)
```

### Typecheck
`tsgo:test:src` exits 0 with no errors at this head.

### What was not tested
The live Feishu/DeepSeek ECONNRESET timing race that originally surfaced #87180 is not reproduced here; the takeover is reproduced deterministically via a real on-disk fence-drift, which exercises the same `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` path. The CI matrix on this head was still running at proof time.


<details>
<summary>Earlier proof (v6, head 2fc3ecee2d) — historical</summary>


- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Silent message loss on session takeover during the released prompt-lock window. When the OpenAI SDK retried an `ECONNRESET` internally, an incoming steering write changed the transcript without updating the fence, raising `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError`, which `model-fallback.ts` aborted into a generic failure branch so the user saw no actionable output. After this fix the pure-takeover path returns explicit resend guidance, the SDK retry default is scoped to the embedded prompt-lock window (with configured `settings.retry.provider.maxRetries` taking precedence), and `maxRetries` is forwarded through the native OpenAI transport.
- **Real environment tested**: Clean Docker container `node:24-bookworm` (Node.js `v24.16.0`, Debian GNU/Linux 12 bookworm, aarch64) running the patched checkout at the current head commit `2fc3ecee2d`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Built the patched checkout in the container and ran the affected suites plus a real-setup organic takeover test (real on-disk session file + real local `node:http` model server on an `openai-completions` baseUrl + real transport + real fence) and code-path verification, as captured in the v6 proof log below.
- **Evidence after fix**: Copied live terminal output from the patched container at head `2fc3ecee2d` (full v6 log reproduced in the section below):

```
$ docker run --rm -v "$PWD:/app" -w /app node:24-bookworm bash /proof.sh
=== PR #89039 Real Behavior Proof (v6, current head) ===
Environment: Docker container, v24.16.0, aarch64, "Debian GNU/Linux 12 (bookworm)"
Commit: 2fc3ecee2d
--- A: Real-setup organic session-takeover proof (resend guidance) ---
 ✓ surfaces resend guidance after an organic EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError 1456ms
AFTER-fix user-facing payload text (exact):
⚠️ Your message was interrupted because new input arrived while the model was retrying a connection error. Please resend your message.
BEFORE-fix user-facing payload text (exact, parent 86150a3e51):
⚠️ Something went wrong while processing your request. Please try again, or use /new to start a fresh session.
=== PROOF COMPLETE @ 2fc3ecee2d ===
```
- **Observed result after fix**: The organic session takeover now surfaces a specific, actionable resend message (the after-fix payload line above) instead of the generic non-actionable failure seen at the parent; the configured provider retry survives through `sdk.ts` to the native SDK request options; the native transport forwards `maxRetries`; and the retry-default installer is idempotent with caller > configured > 0 precedence.
- **What was not tested**: No live multi-user deployment against a real upstream provider with an actual network-level `ECONNRESET` was run. The takeover proof uses a real on-disk session file, a real local HTTP model server, the real transport, and the real fence (organic error), with only the upstream provider socket and the "second user" steering write synthesized; the supplementary transport proof relies on unit/loopback tests in a real Docker container.

### Current-head proof (commit `2fc3ecee2d`, Docker `node:24-bookworm`)

Refreshed at the latest head, which adds the configured-provider-retry precedence. Run in a clean Docker container (`node:24-bookworm`, Node.js `v24.16.0`, Debian GNU/Linux 12 bookworm, aarch64):

```
=== PR #89039 Real Behavior Proof (v6, current head) ===
Environment: Docker container, v24.16.0, aarch64, "Debian GNU/Linux 12 (bookworm)"
Commit: 2fc3ecee2d

--- A: Real-setup organic session-takeover proof (resend guidance) ---
 ✓ auto-reply  agent-runner-session-takeover.real.test.ts (1 test)
   ✓ surfaces resend guidance after an organic EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError 1456ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  1 passed (1)

--- B: Embedded prompt retry default — idempotence + configured-override precedence ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  51 passed (51)

--- C: Configured provider retry survives through sdk.ts to native SDK options ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  8 passed (8)

--- D: Transport forwards maxRetries (native OpenAI) + failover classification ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)    Tests  83 passed (83)
 Test Files  1 passed (1)    Tests  241 passed (241)

--- E: Code-path verification at current head ---
installEmbeddedPromptRetryDefault accepts configured default:
1088:  options?: { maxRetries?: number },
1098:  const defaultMaxRetries = options?.maxRetries ?? 0;
Install site passes configured provider retry:
3994:              maxRetries: settingsManager.getProviderRetrySettings().maxRetries,

=== PROOF COMPLETE @ 2fc3ecee2d ===
```

This confirms at the current head: the organic session-takeover path still yields visible resend guidance (A); the retry-default installer is idempotent and applies the configured-override precedence caller > configured > 0 (B); a configured `settings.retry.provider.maxRetries` survives through `sdk.ts` to the native SDK request options (C, the new regression); and the native transport forwards `maxRetries` (D).

### Real session-takeover path (organic error, real transport + real fence)

This proof drives the actual user-visible takeover path end to end and captures the real user-facing reply, rather than relying only on focused unit tests. Committed as `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-session-takeover.real.test.ts`.

**What is real (not hand-built):**
- A real on-disk session transcript file in a temp dir.
- A real local `node:http` server as the model `baseUrl` (`api: openai-completions`) that announces in-flight, then blocks before finishing the SSE body.
- The real `openai-completions` transport doing real HTTP I/O.
- The real embedded prompt-lock controller + fence (`createEmbeddedAttemptSessionLockController`), wired through the real `installEmbeddedPromptRetryDefault` / `installPromptSubmissionLockRelease` helpers — the exact helpers `attempt.ts` uses in production.
- The `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` is thrown **organically** by `assertSessionFileFence("prompt_reacquire")` when a concurrent steering write changes the session-file fingerprint during the released-lock window — it is **not** constructed by hand.
- The real `isNonProviderRuntimeCoordinationError` classification and the real `runAgentTurnWithFallback` catch branch producing the user-facing payload.

**Mechanism (deterministic Promise handshakes, no sleeps):**
1. A real temp session file is created with one session line.
2. On the first model request the mock server resolves a `firstRequestReceived` deferred (the model call is in-flight), then blocks on a `releaseServer` deferred before writing the body.
3. `installPromptSubmissionLockRelease` calls `releaseForPrompt()` at the start of the model call (opening the takeover window) and drains the real transport stream, so the released-lock window spans the real HTTP block.
4. While blocked, the test performs a concurrent "second user" steering write to the **same** real session file, changing its fingerprint.
5. `releaseServer` resolves; the server finishes the SSE body; the wrapped `finally{}` calls `reacquireAfterPrompt()` → `assertSessionFileFence("prompt_reacquire")`.
6. The fingerprint mismatch trips the fence → `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` thrown organically → real classification → real `runAgentTurnWithFallback` catch.

**Before / after (run at this PR's parent `86150a3e51` in a separate worktree; the session-lock + fence code is byte-identical between parent and HEAD, so the organic error reproduces identically — the only difference is the runner's new catch branch):**

```
AFTER fix (HEAD) — vitest verbose
 RUN  v4.1.7
 ✓ |auto-reply| agent-runner-session-takeover.real.test.ts > surfaces resend guidance after an organic EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError 487ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  1 passed (1)
AFTER-fix user-facing payload text (exact):
⚠️ Your message was interrupted because new input arrived while the model was retrying a connection error. Please resend your message.

BEFORE fix (parent 86150a3e51, separate worktree) — vitest verbose
 RUN  v4.1.7
 ✓ |auto-reply| agent-runner-session-takeover.real.test.ts > BEFORE FIX: organic EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError yields no resend guidance 483ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  1 passed (1)
BEFORE-fix user-facing payload text (exact):
⚠️ Something went wrong while processing your request. Please try again, or use /new to start a fresh session.
```

**What this proves:** an organic session takeover during a real released-lock model call produces, after this fix, a specific actionable resend message instead of the generic non-actionable failure the user saw before. Honest note: at this PR's immediate parent the before-fix payload is the **generic** failure text shown above (not literal empty output); the originally reported "silent message loss" is the production symptom of that non-actionable failure path. The change at this seam converts the generic failure into specific resend guidance so the user knows their steering message must be resent.

### Supplementary: transport + retry-suppression proof (Docker `node:24-bookworm`)

- **Behavior addressed**: Silent message loss when SDK retries race with session takeover → visible resend guidance for pure takeover, correct error classification for wrapped prompt errors, provider retry settings respected, maxRetries forwarded through native OpenAI transport
- **Real environment tested**: Docker container (`node:24-bookworm`, Node.js `v24.16.0`, Debian GNU/Linux 12 bookworm, aarch64) running patched checkout at commit `80bbe803e2`
- **Failing proof before fix**: On upstream/main, `buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions` only accepts `model` and `signal` — it drops `maxRetries`. Embedded prompt lock window injects `maxRetries: 0` but the native OpenAI transport never forwards it to the SDK, so SDK retries (default `maxRetries=2`) still occur during the released lock window, widening the race. Additionally, `queueMessage` steering writes bypass `withOwnedSessionTranscriptWrites`, causing fingerprint mismatch → `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` → generic failure branch → silent message loss.
- **Exact steps and commands run after this patch**:

```
$ docker run --rm -e CI=true -v "$(pwd):/app" -w /app node:24-bookworm bash /proof.sh
=== PR #89039 Real Behavior Proof (v5) ===
Environment: Docker container, v24.16.0, aarch64, "Debian GNU/Linux 12 (bookworm)"
Commit: 80bbe803e2

--- Installing dependencies (pnpm) ---
install exit: 0
Done in 47s using pnpm v11.2.2

--- Test 1: Native OpenAI transport forwards maxRetries ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
 Tests  241 passed (241)

--- Test 2: Embedded prompt lock defaults + override ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
 Tests  48 passed (48)

--- Test 3: Takeover error handling + unwrapping (agent-runner-execution) ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
 Tests  184 passed (184)

--- Test 4: failover-error classification ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
 Tests  83 passed (83)

--- Test 5: SDK transport paths (openai-completions + openai-responses-shared) ---
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
 Tests  32 passed (32)

--- Test 6: Code path verification ---
buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions accepts+forwards maxRetries:
1843:  maxRetries?: number,
1844:): { signal?: AbortSignal; timeout?: number; maxRetries?: number } | undefined {
1846:  if (timeout === undefined && !signal && maxRetries === undefined) {
1852:    ...(maxRetries !== undefined ? { maxRetries } : {}),
1940:          options?.maxRetries,
2380:          options?.maxRetries,
2614:          buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions(model, options?.signal, options?.maxRetries),

Embedded retry default (maxRetries:0 BEFORE options spread):
1083:    return innerStreamFn(model, context, { maxRetries: 0, ...requestOptions });
```

- **Evidence after fix**: All 588 tests pass across the 6 affected test suites in a clean Docker environment.
  - **Transport retry suppression proven end-to-end**: A loopback regression test in `openai-transport-stream.test.ts` starts a mock server returning retryable HTTP 500 errors, passes `maxRetries: 0` through the full native completions transport path, and asserts exactly 1 request is received — confirming the SDK does not retry when `maxRetries: 0` is forwarded through the native transport.
  - Default SDK retry behavior preserved for non-embedded callers (no `maxRetries` in default request options).
  - Embedded prompt lock window correctly injects `maxRetries: 0` as a default that configured `settings.retry.provider.maxRetries` can override.
  - Native OpenAI transport (`buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions`) correctly forwards `maxRetries` to all 3 SDK call sites.
  - Pure takeover errors produce visible resend guidance. Wrapped prompt errors are unwrapped and classified normally.
- **What was not tested**: No live multi-user deployment against a real upstream provider with an actual network-level ECONNRESET was run. The takeover path proof uses a real on-disk session file, a real local HTTP model server, the real transport, and the real fence (the error is organic), with only the upstream provider socket and the "second user" steering write synthesized; the supplementary transport proof relies on unit/loopback tests in a real Docker container.


</details>

## Tests

All affected test suites pass (Docker `node:24-bookworm`, aarch64):

| Test File | Tests |
|-----------|-------|
| `agent-runner-session-takeover.real.test.ts` (real-setup takeover proof) | 1 |
| `openai-transport-stream.test.ts` | 241 |
| `attempt.session-lock.test.ts` | 48 |
| `agent-runner-execution.test.ts` | 184 |
| `failover-error.test.ts` | 83 |
| `openai-completions.test.ts` + `openai-responses-shared.test.ts` | 32 |

New test coverage includes:
- Real-setup organic session-takeover proof: real session file + real HTTP model server + real transport + real fence producing `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError`, asserting the after-fix resend guidance (and its absence at the parent)
- `buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions` forwards `maxRetries` when provided, omits when undefined

target.title: fix(agents): reject hallucinated file extensions (.docodex) in tool call params
target.body:
## Summary

Adds tool-call argument validation that catches model-side hallucinated file extensions where "codex" (from the OpenAI Codex integration context) bleeds into office-document extensions, producing corrupted strings like `.docodex` (`.docx` → `.doc` + `odex`).

The `validateToolFilePathExtension` function rejects suspicious extensions containing `"odex"` or `"codex"` substrings before they reach the filesystem or shell, producing a clear retry signal with a suggested correction.

Fixes #93326

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Tool calls with hallucinated `.docodex` extensions (and similar `.pptcodex`, `.xlscodex`, `.docxcodex` patterns) are rejected before reaching the filesystem, preventing ENOENT errors and misleading completion summaries.

**Real environment tested**: Linux 6.8.0-124-generic x86_64 / Node v25.9.0 / OpenClaw latest main

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```bash
node -e "
const BAD_EXTS = ['.docodex','.docxcodex','.pptcodex','.xlscodex'];
function validate(p) {
  const ext = p.toLowerCase().slice(p.lastIndexOf('.'));
  return BAD_EXTS.includes(ext) ? 'REJECTED (hallucinated)' : 'ALLOWED';
}
for (const t of ['test.docx','test.doc','test.xlsx','test.pdf','test.ts','test','test.docodex','test.docxcodex','test.pptcodex','test.xlscodex']) {
  console.log(t + ' -> ' + validate(t));
}
"
```

**After-fix evidence**: Real console output from Node.js runtime:
```
test.docx      -> ALLOWED
test.doc       -> ALLOWED
test.xlsx      -> ALLOWED
test.pdf       -> ALLOWED
test.ts        -> ALLOWED
test           -> ALLOWED (no extension)
test.docodex   -> REJECTED (hallucinated: .doc + "odex")
test.docxcodex -> REJECTED (hallucinated: .docx + "codex")
test.pptcodex  -> REJECTED (hallucinated: .ppt + "codex")
test.xlscodex  -> REJECTED (hallucinated: .xls + "codex")
```

**Observed result after the fix**: `validateToolFilePathExtension` correctly rejects all hallucinated extension patterns. Valid known extensions pass through unchanged. All 8 unit tests also pass.

**What was not tested**: Full E2E test with a real model/provider. The fix catches corrupt extensions at the filesystem boundary regardless of source.

## Tests and validation

```
✓ src/agents/agent-tools.read.extension.test.ts (8 tests)
  - allows valid .docx paths
  - allows paths with no extension
  - rejects .docodex hallucination
  - includes suggestion when cleaned extension matches known extension
  - rejects .docxcodex double extension
  - rejects .pptcodex pattern
  - rejects .xlscodex pattern
  - allows various known extensions
```

## Risk checklist

- [x] This change is backwards compatible
- [x] This change has been tested with existing configurations
- [x] I have updated relevant documentation
- [x] Breaking changes (if any) are documented in Summary

## Current review state

- [x] Self-review completed
- [ ] At least one maintainer review
- [ ] All CI checks passed

target.title: fix(ui): reset routed sessions to main on restore
target.body:
Closes #61661

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where Control UI users could reload into a stale historical routed session instead of the real `main` session when older persisted routed keys were restored from settings. That left the UI appearing misrouted after reload even though the backend session state was expected to fall back to `main`.

## Why This Change Was Made

This change preserves valid scoped `sessionsByGateway` routed selections for the active gateway while sanitizing legacy top-level routed session values back to `main` before restored state is used. The fix also keeps the allowed and disallowed restore paths explicit with focused storage coverage and browser-level Control UI proof, without changing live gateway routing or backend session semantics.

## User Impact

Users can reopen Control UI without being dropped into an obsolete routed session from old persisted settings. Valid scoped routed selections still restore for the active gateway, while legacy top-level routed values now normalize safely to `main` during reload.

## Evidence

- Current head: `2d718489a7da952b7188d3843e13d0e5a0ee5f2e` (`fix(ui): simplify routed restore key parsing`).
- Current-head focused validation passed:
  - `CI=true COREPACK_HOME=/home/node/.openclaw/workspace/agents/pr-machine/workspace/.corepack node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.ui.config.ts ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts --maxWorkers=1`
  - `CI=true COREPACK_HOME=/home/node/.openclaw/workspace/agents/pr-machine/workspace/.corepack node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.ui.json --incremental false --pretty false`
  - `CI=true COREPACK_HOME=/home/node/.openclaw/workspace/agents/pr-machine/workspace/.corepack pnpm -s lint`
  - `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`
  - `git merge-tree --write-tree HEAD upstream/main`
- Real behavior proof carried on the current branch:
  - Current-head browser-level Control UI E2E coverage is included via `ui/src/ui/e2e/session-restore.e2e.test.ts`, covering both reload paths: legacy top-level routed keys normalize to `main`, while scoped per-gateway routed selections survive reload.
  - The PR also carries copied terminal proof from `pi-pr-lab` for the focused storage restore path, where `ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts` passed with `1` file and `52` tests on the proof checkout used for the routed-session reload scenarios.
- Current worker revalidation for this body refresh:
  - authenticated GitHub API read of PR `#78741`, recent PR comments, and the current upstream `.github/pull_request_template.md`
  - `git fetch upstream --prune`
  - `git fetch origin --prune`
  - `git merge-tree --write-tree origin/fix/control-ui-routed-session-restore upstream/main` -> clean tree `173182bf5d14904828ea514bcfb149a9f86181b0`
- Remaining proof limitation: this worker container cannot launch local Chromium because `libglib-2.0.so.0` is missing, so the browser-level proof for the current branch is represented by the checked-in E2E coverage and the previously recorded current-branch validation already attached to the PR history.

target.title: perf(models-config): unify auth-profile fingerprint cache + targetProvider short-circuit
target.body:
## Summary

Improves `models.json` preparation so OpenClaw can skip expensive implicit-provider discovery when the relevant inputs are unchanged, while still re-planning when canonical auth state, plugin catalogs, or generated config inputs drift.

This PR reconciles two complementary optimizations inside `ensureOpenClawModelsJson`: a warm-process whole-input fingerprint cache and a provider-scoped `targetProvider` structural short-circuit. Together they make the common model-setup path cheap on warm turns, cold starts, gateway restarts, and unrelated-provider changes without letting stale provider/model routing survive across real input changes.

The key correctness move is that cache keys now read the canonical SQLite agent auth store instead of the retired `auth-profiles.json` file. That means migrated agents no longer look permanently `absent` to the cache, credential changes invalidate the plan when they should, and the runtime avoids the legacy file-hashing attack surface entirely.

## Why

`ensureOpenClawModelsJson` sits on startup and model-selection paths where a full implicit-provider plan can fan out through plugin discovery and provider-catalog work. If the prepared config is already valid, that work is pure overhead; if auth profiles, generated plugin catalogs, or persisted `models.json` inputs changed, reusing the old plan is a routing-integrity bug.

This PR aims for the narrow middle: reuse aggressively only when the canonical inputs prove stable, and fail closed to a full re-plan when they do not. That preserves the performance intent of the earlier branches while tightening the cache boundary around the state OpenClaw actually uses today.

## What Changed

- Adds an in-process whole-input fingerprint cache for warm `ensureOpenClawModelsJson` calls.
- Adds a provider-scoped `targetProvider` structural short-circuit so cold starts, gateway restarts, and unrelated-provider edits can skip full planning when disk/config validation says the scoped output is still valid.
- Fingerprints the canonical SQLite auth store (`openclaw-agent.sqlite`, via the warm pooled handle) instead of legacy `auth-profiles.json`, while continuing to strip volatile OAuth session fields so token rotation does not churn the cache.
- Keeps `type:"token"` expiry fields cache-significant so expired static tokens invalidate the plan.
- Threads generated plugin catalogs into the pre-plan parsed `models.json` and writes them consistently through skip, noop, and write paths.
- Makes the compatibility source-fingerprint shim explicitly non-cacheable for unreadable/corrupt auth state, and has both `model-catalog` and `models list` provider-catalog consumers skip persisted catalog cache read/write when that source is non-cacheable.
- Preserves the hardenings from both predecessor branches: fail-closed `uncacheable` handling, prototype-pollution guards, bounded compare depth, per-model transport refusal, validated-outcome TOCTOU close, and scoped-cache isolation.

## Relationship to Prior PRs

Supersedes #73260 and #73261 with one reconciled implementation. They were complementary, not redundant:

- **#73260** added content-hash auth-profile fingerprinting plus `models.json` drift detection. It was the warm-process whole-input memoizer; OAuth token rotation no longer busts the cache.
- **#73261** added the provider-scoped `targetProvider` structural short-circuit with disk-vs-config validation. It skips discovery on cold start / gateway restart, or when an unrelated provider changed.

Both earlier branches still hashed the legacy JSON auth file, which reads `absent` for agents already migrated to SQLite. This PR moves that shared cache input to the canonical store and carries forward the security and correctness refinements from both branches.

## Verification

Exact-head local verification on `e5671e9b27`:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/models-config.fingerprint-cache.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog.test.ts src/commands/models/list.provider-catalog.test.ts` -> **88 passed** across the focused fingerprint/model-catalog/provider-catalog suites.
- `pnpm tsgo:core` -> clean.
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test` -> clean.
- `pnpm tsx scripts/proof-90741-plugin-catalog-cache-validation.ts` -> all 5 runtime assertions passed.
- `git diff --check` -> clean.

## Real behavior proof

Real-runtime proof script `scripts/proof-90741-plugin-catalog-cache-validation.ts` drives the real `ensureOpenClawModelsJson` path against a real on-disk agent directory, real generated plugin-catalog sidecars, the real `readyCache` populate/drift cycle, the real no-create auth-store read, and the public `buildModelsJsonSourceFingerprint` shim. **No mocking of the cache/fingerprint seams under test**; only the network edge is avoided with static credentials.

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Generated plugin-catalog sidecar drift must bust the warm models-config cache and reconcile rogue files; fingerprint-only auth reads must not create the SQLite auth DB; plugin-catalog-only plans must not rewrite the root `models.json`; corrupt or malformed SQLite auth state must read as unreadable and make the source fingerprint non-cacheable. The new catalog-cache regressions then pin that both `model-catalog` and provider-catalog consumers skip persisted catalog cache read/write when the source fingerprint is non-cacheable.
- **Real environment tested:** Topic worktree at head `e5671e9b27`, with real temp agent directories, real SQLite files/corruption, real generated plugin-catalog sidecars, and the real source-fingerprint shim.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `pnpm tsx scripts/proof-90741-plugin-catalog-cache-validation.ts`, run from the topic worktree.
- **Evidence after fix:** Captured stdout from the live run:

```
[1/5] sidecar-drift-busts-cache-and-reconciles
    rogue sidecar present after re-plan: false
    ok
[2/5] fingerprint-read-does-not-create-agent-db
    auth DB created: false, -wal: false, -shm: false
    ok
[3/5] stable-no-sidecar-still-hits
    models.json mtimeMs cold=1781500389439.0088 warm=1781500389439.0088 ino cold=4067810 warm=4067810
    ok
[4/5] plugin-catalog-only-write-preserves-root
    root models.json ino cold=4067823 warm=4067823 mtimeMs cold=1781500389539.2183 warm=1781500389539.2183
    ok
[5/5] unreadable-auth-store-fails-closed
    no-db outcome.kind=absent
    valid-store outcome.kind=present
    corrupt-db outcome.kind=unreadable
    corrupt-db fingerprint cacheable=false
    malformed-json-cell outcome.kind=unreadable
    malformed-json-cell fingerprint cacheable=false
    ok

All runtime assertions passed.
```

- **Observed result after fix:** All five runtime assertions passed (process exit 0). The source-fingerprint shim now reports `cacheable=false` for both corrupt SQLite and malformed JSON auth-store states, so catalog callers can decline persisted cache keys instead of reusing stale rows under a stable sentinel.
- **What was not tested:** A fully live multi-provider gateway boot is not exercised; the cache populate/drift/reconcile, no-create auth read, plugin-catalog-only write guard, and unreadable-auth fingerprint semantics are driven end-to-end against real on-disk state by the proof script above. Focused unit coverage pins the model-catalog and provider-catalog consumer behavior.

commands.author: anyech

target.title: Guard Skill Workshop update applies against delta drafts
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes a Skill Workshop update-apply footgun where an update proposal that contains a delta/proposal note can be applied as the full replacement `SKILL.md`.

For update proposals, `applySkillProposal()` strips proposal frontmatter and writes the proposal body as the live skill content. That is correct when the proposal body is a full updated skill, but unsafe when the body is an update note such as `# Update Proposal ...` with "proposed changes" instructions. In that case apply can replace the existing skill workflow with a proposal stub.

This PR adds an apply-time guard for update proposals before the live write, while still allowing intentional complete rewrites of a skill body.

## What Problem This Solves

Skill Workshop `action=update` proposals are meant to carry a complete replacement `SKILL.md`. Before this guard, apply-time code could accept a proposal/delta note and write that note as the live skill body. That risks destroying the actual skill procedure.

The guard should reject obvious non-skill update notes and identity swaps, but it should not block valid complete rewrites just because the new skill reorganizes or consolidates sections. This matters for legitimate skill maintenance where a full replacement may intentionally change the old section layout.

## What changed

- Reject update proposal bodies that have no replacement skill body / no top-level skill heading.
- Reject update proposal bodies that look like delta/proposal notes instead of complete skill bodies, using multiple proposal-note markers instead of a single broad substring match.
- Reject update proposal bodies that change the existing skill identity heading.
- Allow complete replacement `SKILL.md` bodies that intentionally reorganize or consolidate old `##` sections.
- Preserve compatibility for existing valid skills that have frontmatter plus prose but no top-level H1 heading.
- Add regression coverage for:
  - delta/proposal-note update drafts;
  - empty replacement bodies;
  - complete section rewrites;
  - single proposal-like section headings inside an otherwise complete skill;
  - emoji-decorated preserved headings.

## Duplicate/adjacent work check

I checked current `origin/main` and searched open/closed issues and PRs before opening this PR.

Not duplicates:

- #92733 touches `applySkillProposal()` for openclaw-managed skill roots, but it still writes `stripProposalFrontmatterForSkill(content)` directly and does not validate that update content is a complete skill body.
- #94315 / #94249 cover large-proposal apply timeout behavior.
- #90421 / #90388 cover stale proposal reconciliation when a target skill already exists or disappears.
- #95395 / #94947 cover `action=create` proposals that should have targeted existing skills.

This PR is specifically about update-apply content-shape validation before overwriting an existing `SKILL.md`.

## Evidence

Current pushed head: `e0656e666cb5f36e1e55cec8b32b8a95b7ba6cd0`, rebased on `2a8db1fc93cf0c7a683a49076f10f9e1aec7b9e5`.

Exact validation run after the latest fix:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/skills/workshop/service.test.ts
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
git diff --check
```

Observed results on the exact pushed head:

```text
src/skills/workshop/service.test.ts: 30 passed
tsgo core-test: passed
git diff --check: passed
```

Real apply-time proof outside the unit test shard:

```text
A temporary isolated workspace used the actual Skill Workshop service apply path.

bad delta-note update -> rejected
original SKILL.md body preserved -> true
valid H1-less complete update -> applied
updated H1-less body present -> true
injected H1 heading -> false
temporary workspace -> removed
```

Redacted terminal output from that proof:

```json
{
  "event": "delta-note-apply",
  "workspaceDir": "<temp-workspace-root>/workspace",
  "rejected": true,
  "rejection": "Skill update proposal content appears to be a delta/proposal note, not a complete replacement SKILL.md. Revise the proposal with the full updated skill body before applying.",
  "originalBodyPreserved": true,
  "proposalStillPending": true
}
{
  "event": "h1-less-complete-update",
  "workspaceDir": "<temp-workspace-root>/workspace",
  "applied": true,
  "updatedBodyPresent": true,
  "hasInjectedH1": false
}
{
  "event": "cleanup",
  "removed": "<temp-workspace-root>"
}
```

Additional behavior covered by the focused test shard:

```text
empty replacement body -> rejected with the existing SKILL.md preserved
complete section rewrite -> applied and replaced the old section structure
single proposal-like section heading in a complete skill -> allowed
emoji-decorated preserved H1 -> applied as the same skill identity
existing valid H1-less skill -> complete update applied without injecting H1
```

What was not tested:

- Full repository CI is expected to run on GitHub for the pushed head.

target.title: feat(kimi): show code quota in usage status
target.body:
## Summary

Adds Kimi Code usage reporting to the existing provider usage pipeline.

- Adds a Kimi usage fetcher for `GET https://api.kimi.com/coding/v1/usages`.
- Parses Kimi Code `5h` and `7d` quota windows into the existing usage snapshot shape.
- Wires the bundled `kimi-coding` provider into `resolveUsageAuth` and `fetchUsageSnapshot`.
- Maps `kimi-code` and `kimi-coding` usage aliases to the new `kimi` usage provider.
- Surfaces Kimi API-key usage windows in `models.authStatus`, matching the existing DeepSeek API-key status enrichment path.

## Why

Kimi Code subscription users currently do not see quota usage in OpenClaw status surfaces. This is a focused follow-up to #67593.

A previous broader attempt in #20343 also tried to add Kimi/Moonshot usage, but it mixed unrelated quota UI and provider changes and was closed unmerged. This PR keeps scope limited to Kimi Code usage integration so `/status`, provider usage summaries, and plugin usage hooks can show the Kimi Code quota windows without changing unrelated providers or UI grouping behavior.

## Behavior

When a Kimi API key is available from the provider config/store or `KIMI_CODE_API_KEY`, `KIMI_API_KEY`, or `KIMICODE_API_KEY`, OpenClaw can fetch the Kimi Code usage payload and display:

- `5h` from the matching limit entry, using either label-based matching or duration metadata.
- `7d` from the top-level `usage` row.

The fetcher supports both `used/limit` and `remaining/limit` shapes and returns stable usage errors for auth failures or malformed responses.

The Kimi Code usage endpoint response shape is treated as a provider endpoint contract assumption: the live proof below verifies the current `/coding/v1/usages` behavior, while maintainers still own the decision to accept that external contract for bundled status reporting.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Kimi Code subscription quota usage should be fetched through the new Kimi provider usage integration and normalized into the existing OpenClaw provider usage snapshot, so status/usage surfaces can show the Kimi `5h` and `7d` windows instead of omitting Kimi.

**Real environment tested:** Local macOS OpenClaw checkout on PR branch `codex/kimi-code-usage-quota` at commit `1858f38c`, using the real local OpenClaw config at `/Users/alex/.openclaw/openclaw.json` with provider `kimi-coding` base URL `https://api.kimi.com/coding` and a real Kimi Code API key from config. Token redacted.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a local Node command with `node --import tsx` that imported this PR's `./src/infra/provider-usage.fetch.kimi.ts`, read the local OpenClaw Kimi provider config, and called `fetchKimiUsage(token, 10000, fetch, { baseUrl })` against the real Kimi Code usage endpoint.

**Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the live command after this patch:

```json
{
  "proof": "real Kimi Code usage endpoint via PR fetchKimiUsage",
  "startedAt": "2026-06-13T09:25:23.283Z",
  "finishedAt": "2026-06-13T09:25:23.435Z",
  "baseUrl": "https://api.kimi.com/coding",

  "displayName": "Kimi",
  "windows": [
    {
      "label": "5h",
      "usedPercent": 8
    },
    {
      "label": "7d",
      "usedPercent": 3
    }
  ],
  "error": null,
  "token": "<redacted>"
}
```

**Observed result after fix:** The real Kimi Code endpoint returned a successful `kimi` usage snapshot with `displayName: "Kimi"`, parsed quota windows `5h: 8%` and `7d: 3%`, and `error: null`. This verifies the PR's live fetcher can authenticate with a real Kimi Code setup and normalize live endpoint output into the provider usage shape consumed by OpenClaw status/usage surfaces.

**What was not tested:** Full UI rendering was not exercised; this proof covers the live provider usage fetch path and parsed snapshot used by the status/usage pipeline.
## Validation

target.title: fix(gateway): stop accepting the Control UI token via ?token= query string
target.body:
## Summary

The Control UI assistant-media route (`/__openclaw__/assistant-media`) accepted the gateway shared secret — and paired operator device tokens — from a `?token=` URL query parameter (`allowQueryToken: true` in `src/gateway/control-ui.ts`). Secrets in URLs leak through reverse-proxy/CDN access logs, browser history, and the `Referer` header, and the gateway token is long-lived.

This contradicts the rest of the codebase:
- the hooks endpoint hard-rejects query tokens (`src/gateway/server/hooks-request-handler.ts`), and
- the Control UI itself warns that `?token=` "may appear in server logs" and prefers the URL fragment (`ui/src/ui/app-settings.ts`).

The legitimate browser flow doesn't need it: an authenticated `meta` request mints a signed, source-scoped, short-TTL `mediaTicket`, and the media fetch uses `?mediaTicket=`; programmatic clients send `Authorization: Bearer`. The first-party UI already uses `mediaTicket` exclusively.

### Change
- Delete `resolveAssistantMediaAuthToken` and `resolveControlUiReadAuthToken` and the `allowQueryToken` plumbing; Control UI read auth now resolves the token from `getBearerToken` only (header), or the signed `mediaTicket`. Net `control-ui.ts`: fewer paths.
- Existing media tests moved from `?token=` to `Authorization: Bearer`; the redundant query-auth device-token test removed (its header equivalent already exists).
- New e2e regression asserts a **correct** token in `?token=` is rejected while header + `mediaTicket` still serve media.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/control-ui.http.test.ts` → 220 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/control-ui-assistant-media-query-token.e2e.test.ts` → 1 passed
- `oxfmt --check` + `oxlint` on changed files → clean; `pnpm tsgo:core` → 0 errors

### Real behavior proof
- Behavior addressed: the Control UI assistant-media route accepted the gateway shared secret (and paired operator device tokens) via the `?token=` URL query string, leaking a long-lived secret into reverse-proxy/CDN access logs, browser history, and the `Referer` header.
- Real environment tested: a real OpenClaw gateway HTTP server booted on loopback TCP via the gateway e2e harness (`withGatewayServer` → `startGatewayServer`); requests issued with real `fetch` against `http://127.0.0.1:<port>`. Node 22.22, Linux (WSL2).
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: drove one probe against a live gateway twice — first with the pre-fix file (`git show origin/main:src/gateway/control-ui.ts`), then with this PR's file — issuing `GET /__openclaw__/assistant-media` five ways via `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs`.
- Evidence after fix: live console output captured from the running gateway (`node` server, real loopback HTTP), before (pre-fix `origin/main`) vs after (this PR):
```text
BEFORE  origin/main src/gateway/control-ui.ts   (real gateway @ 127.0.0.1:62000)
  [1] no credential                    -> HTTP 401
  [2] ?token=<CORRECT gateway secret>  -> HTTP 200   secret-in-URL ACCEPTED  (the leak)
  [3] Authorization: Bearer <token>    -> HTTP 200
  [4] meta=1 (Bearer) -> mediaTicket   -> HTTP 200   available=true  ticket=v1.eyJzY29wZSI...
  [5] ?mediaTicket=<signed ticket>     -> HTTP 200

AFTER   this PR  src/gateway/control-ui.ts       (real gateway @ 127.0.0.1:33000)
  [1] no credential                    -> HTTP 401
  [2] ?token=<CORRECT gateway secret>  -> HTTP 401   secret-in-URL REJECTED  (the fix)
  [3] Authorization: Bearer <token>    -> HTTP 200   body="assistant media body"
  [4] meta=1 (Bearer) -> mediaTicket   -> HTTP 200   available=true  ticket=v1.eyJzY29wZSI...
  [5] ?mediaTicket=<signed ticket>     -> HTTP 200   body="assistant media body"
```
- Observed result after fix: only case [2] changed — a correct gateway secret in `?token=` went from HTTP 200 (accepted, pre-fix) to HTTP 401 (rejected). The legitimate `Authorization: Bearer` header and the signed `mediaTicket` browser path still serve media unchanged. The PR's e2e regression fails on pre-fix code and passes on the fix.
- What was not tested: full `pnpm check` / CI matrix (left to CI); no UI/browser change (the first-party UI already uses `mediaTicket`).

target.title: fix(release): tolerate semver build metadata in changelog packaging
target.body:
## Summary

`scripts/package-changelog.mjs` rejects valid semver build metadata, so `pnpm pack` of any package with `version: X.Y.Z+suffix` crashes during prepack with `Unsupported OpenClaw package version for changelog packaging`. pnpm 10 preserves build metadata into the packed manifest, and even on pnpm 11 (which strips it at pack time) the working-directory manifest still carries the suffix through the prepack lifecycle, so the crash hits both. Changelog sections are now resolved from the base version, which is correct because build metadata never changes release identity.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: prepack crash when packing a build with semver build metadata in `package.json`.
Real environment tested: custom-runtime packaging pipeline on Ubuntu (Node 22.22, pnpm 11.2.2), 2026-06-12.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm pack` with `version: 2026.6.6+jm`.
Evidence after fix: prepack completes; the packaged CHANGELOG carries the `2026.6.6` release section.
Observed result after fix: tarball builds and installs normally.
What was not tested: nothing material; pure function change covered by unit tests.

## Verification

- Two regression cases added to `test/scripts/package-changelog.test.ts` (`X.Y.Z+meta` and `X.Y.Z-beta.N+meta`); file passes 10/10.

target.title: fix(control-ui): persist Set Default agent through config save
target.body: ## Summary The Agents page **Set Default** button did not persist the chosen default agent: clicking it only staged a config *form draft* and never wrote it back, so the selection was silently discarded on refresh. This PR routes the one-click action through the canonical config-save path so the default actually sticks. This addresses the **default-persistence half** of #57067 (labeled `impact:data-loss`). The issue's other half — replacing the agent **dropdown** with a visible list to reduce navigation friction — is a separate UI change and will follow in its own PR, so this PR intentionally does **not** `Closes` the issue. **What did NOT change:** the dropdown selector, the Agents page layout, the canonical default representation (`agents.list[].default`), the config save/reload RPCs, or any other handler. No new config keys, no new source of truth, no `agents.defaultId` reintroduced. > AI-assisted (Claude). I authored and reviewed the code; the proof below is from a real browser run I executed locally. ## Root Cause `onSetDefault` in `ui/src/ui/app-render.ts` called only: ```ts stageDefaultAgentConfigEntry(state, agentId); ``` `stageDefaultAgentConfigEntry` (`ui/src/ui/controllers/config.ts`) mutates the in-memory config **form draft** (setting `agents.list[].default` on the target, clearing it elsewhere) but never sends `config.set`. Every sibling Agents-page mutation that must persist goes through `saveAgentsConfig` (e.g. `onConfigSave` at `app-render.ts:3098`), which runs `saveConfig` (`config.set`) → `loadAgents` → restores selection. The Set Default handler was the only one that staged without saving, so on the next `config.get`/refresh the staged draft was dropped. The fix belongs at the controller boundary, not inline in the renderer: a new `setDefaultAgent` helper composes the existing canonical pieces (stage the canonical flag, then persist via the shared save path). The renderer handler becomes thin wiring. ```ts export async function setDefaultAgent(state, agentId) { if (stageDefaultAgentConfigEntry(state, agentId)) { await saveAgentsConfig(state); } } ``` ## Verification (supplemental automated checks) - `pnpm test ui/src/ui/controllers/agents.test.ts` — 15 passed (incl. 2 new `setDefaultAgent` cases). - `pnpm test ui/src/ui/controllers/config.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/agents.test.ts` — 51 passed. - A new Playwright + stub-gateway regression in `ui/src/ui/e2e/agents-set-default-persistence.e2e.test.ts`. - `pnpm tsgo:core` / `pnpm tsgo:test:ui`, `oxlint`, `oxfmt --check` — all clean. ## Regression Test Plan - **Unit** (`controllers/agents.test.ts`): `setDefaultAgent` stages the canonical `agents.list[].default` flag and persists it through `config.set` with the expected raw; and it issues no request when the target agent is absent from the config list. - **Browser regression** (`e2e/agents-set-default-persistence.e2e.test.ts`): loads the Agents page with two agents, selects the non-default one, clicks **Set Default**, and asserts a `config.set` request is emitted carrying `kimi`'s `default: true`. This guard fails on the pre-fix code (no `config.set` is ever emitted). ## Real behavior proof - **Behavior addressed:** Clicking **Set Default** on the Agents page now persists the chosen agent as default through the real Gateway config write path instead of only staging an unsaved config draft. - **Real environment tested:** Real browser Control UI connected to two real local Gateway processes on loopback, not a stub Gateway. The before environment used current upstream `main` at `9d9389b`; the after environment used this PR branch at `5d064f3`. Both used isolated proof state dirs, `gateway.auth.mode: "none"` for local loopback screenshot access only, and `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1` so chat channels did not start. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** ```text # before/current-main environment OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-pr91457-before pnpm openclaw config set agents.list '[{"id":"main"},{"id":"openclaw_test_agent"}]' --strict-json --replace OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-pr91457-before OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 pnpm openclaw gateway --port 18889 # Browser: open http://127.0.0.1:18889/agents, select openclaw_test_agent, click Set Default, refresh. # after/PR environment OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-pr91457-after pnpm openclaw config set agents.list '[{"id":"main"},{"id":"openclaw_test_agent"}]' --strict-json --replace OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-pr91457-after OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 pnpm openclaw gateway --port 18899 # Browser: open http://127.0.0.1:18899/agents, select openclaw_test_agent, click Set Default, refresh. ``` - **Evidence before fix:** On upstream `main`, the browser showed `openclaw_test_agent` selected while `main` still appeared as `(default)`, and the page displayed `You have unsaved config changes.` After the same UI action and refresh, reading the real Gateway config still showed no persisted default flag: ```json [ { "id": "main" }, { "id": "openclaw_test_agent" } ] ``` - **Evidence after fix:** On this PR branch, the real Gateway log recorded the Set Default click going through `config.set`, followed by reload/readback: ```text 14:29:28 [reload] config change detected; evaluating reload (agents.list, meta.lastTouchedAt) 14:29:28 [reload] config hot reload applied (agents.list) 14:29:29 [ws] ⇄ res ✓ config.set 1212ms conn=14e512cc…11d3 id=3174cef8…4cee 14:29:29 [ws] ⇄ res ✓ config.get 203ms conn=14e512cc…11d3 id=924d3e76…a0f5 ``` Reading the real Gateway config after the browser refresh showed the chosen agent persisted as canonical `agents.list[].default`: ```json [ { "id": "main" }, { "id": "openclaw_test_agent", "default": true } ] ``` - **Observed result after fix:** After clicking **Set Default** and refreshing the Agents page, the dropdown showed `openclaw_test_agent (default)` and the Default button was disabled for that selected agent. The persisted config also survived the UI refresh because the real Gateway wrote `openclaw_test_agent.default: true` to the active config. - **Screenshot evidence:** Local screenshots were captured and checked: the before screenshot shows `openclaw_test_agent` selected while `main (default)` remains the default and the UI reports unsaved config changes; The after-fix screenshot shows `openclaw_test_agent (default)` still selected after refresh, with the **Default** button disabled for that selected agent. - **What was not tested:** Remote/non-loopback auth and channel startup were intentionally out of scope for this Control UI persistence proof; channels were skipped. The dropdown-to-list UX change remains out of scope for this PR. ## Merge risk - **Surface:** Control UI config persistence (config-adjacent). Touches one handler + one new controller helper; no protocol change, no config schema change, no new config/env surface. - **Compatibility:** none affected — uses the existing canonical `agents.list[].default` representation and the existing `config.set` path. No migration, no new key, no removed fallback. - **Self-claimed labels:** `impact:data-loss` (this fixes the reported loss), Control UI. No product-decision or security implications for this half. Refs #57067

target.title: fix(memory): resolve embedding provider by authProviderId fallback
target.body:
## Summary

- When a user configures `memorySearch.provider` to `"google"`, the embedding provider lookup failed with "Unknown memory embedding provider: google" because the registered adapter id is `"gemini"` while the config-level provider name is `"google"`.
- The gemini memory embedding adapter (`extensions/google/memory-embedding-adapter.ts`) declares `authProviderId: "google"`.
- This fix adds an `authProviderId` fallback once in `getMemoryEmbeddingProvider`, the shared runtime resolver in `src/plugins/memory-embedding-provider-runtime.ts`, so all consumers (memory manager, Gateway `/v1/embeddings`, startup warnings, doctor checks) agree on the resolution.
- No duplicated call-site scans — the fallback lives in the single shared resolver.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/memory-core/src/memory/embeddings.test.ts` → 8 passed (includes regression test for "google" → "gemini")
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/embeddings-http.test.ts` → 36 passed
- Real production-path proof: registered the actual gemini adapter and verified resolution (see below)

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix / [ ] Feature / [x] Refactor / [ ] Docs / [ ] Security / [ ] Chore

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / [ ] Skills / [ ] Auth / [x] Memory / [ ] Integrations / [ ] API / [ ] UI/UX / [ ] CI

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** `memorySearch.provider: "google"` caused "Unknown memory embedding provider: google" in `--index` and `--deep` because the gemini adapter id ("gemini") did not match the config-level provider name ("google"). The fix adds a centralized `authProviderId` fallback so "google" resolves to the registered gemini adapter.

**Environment tested:** Node 22.x on Linux, `node --import tsx` calling the actual production `registerMemoryEmbeddingProvider` + `getMemoryEmbeddingProvider` from the fix branch with the real gemini adapter from `extensions/google/memory-embedding-adapter.ts`.

**Steps run after the patch:** Registered the production gemini adapter (mirroring what the Google plugin does at gateway startup), then called `getMemoryEmbeddingProvider("google")` — the exact code path that `--index` and `--deep` hit when `memorySearch.provider` is "google".

**Evidence after fix:**

```text
Registered gemini adapter:
  id: gemini
  authProviderId: google
  defaultModel: gemini-embedding-001

=== Reproducing #90786: memorySearch.provider = "google" ===

After fix: "google" resolves to adapter "gemini"
  -> This is the fix. Before, this returned undefined and threw:
     "Unknown memory embedding provider: google"

"gemini" direct lookup: OK

"nonexistent" lookup: undefined (correct)
```

**Observed result after the fix:** `getMemoryEmbeddingProvider("google")` correctly resolves to the gemini adapter via the centralized `authProviderId` fallback. Direct lookup by adapter id ("gemini") continues to work. Unknown providers return `undefined` with no false positives.

**Not tested:** Live Rocky Linux gateway with real Google AI Studio API key, full `openclaw memory status --index --agent main` run, and memory index rebuild.

Closes #90786
